About: It is about political undertones in music.
It is about criticizing the Russian government.
It is about taboo topics such as death.
Affects: It affects the duo's underage fans.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the electronic music genre.
ConsistsOf: It consists of Anastasia Kreslina and Nikolay Kostilev.
CreatedBy: It was created by Anastasia Kreslina and Nikolay Kostilev.
HasA: It has political undertones in their music.
It has a mixture of musical genres.
It has a simultaneous emphasis on both audio and visual components.
It has lyrics that dealt with taboo topics such as death.
It has vocals performed the role of an "instrument" in earlier work.
It has semantic component prevailing in Russian-language texts.
It has references to Russian folk art emphasized in Russian-language texts.
It has tracks in English, Russian, and mixed English and Russian.
It has scripts, direction, editing, and processing of videos done by themselves.
It has photographs and cover art for releases created by themselves.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by the political undertones in their music.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by public pressure.
It was influenced by Western media.
IsA: It is a Russian electronic music duo.
It is a first project released in 2014.
It is the first video work.
It is their first album in Russian.
It is another Russian-language longplay.
It is the band's third album in Russian.
It is an English- and Russian-language album.
It is the second Russian-language album.
It is a music video.
It is a performance.
KnownFor: It is known for political undertones in their music.
It is known for censorship of their music in 2019.
It is known for the video for "Grustnaya Suka" which quickly became a hit.
It is known for the video for "Smerti Bolshe Net" being IC3PEAK's most viewed music video.
It is known for the video for "Smerti Bolshe Net" having wide political resonance.
MemberOf: It is a member of Forbes magazine's "30 under 30" nominees.
OccurredAt: It occurred at Novosibirsk Central Railway Station.
OccurredDuring: It occurred during their stay in Brazil.
PartOf: It is part of Electronica Records.
It is part of Manimal Vinyl.
It is part of Bring Me the Horizon.
It is part of Kim Dracula.
PerformedBy: It was performed by IC3PEAK in European cities.
It was performed by the duo in South America.
PublishedAs: It was published as the EP Substances.
It was published as the EP Vacuum.
It was published as the album Сладкая Жизнь.
It was published as the album СКАЗКА.
It was published as the album До Свидания.
It was published as the album Kiss OF Death.
It was published as the album Coming Home.
It was published as the single "Ellipse".
It was published as the single "I’ll Be Found" remixes.
It was published as the single "Really Really".
PublishedIn: It was published in mixed English and Russian.
UsedIn: It is used in music videos.
It is used in Berlin Music Video Awards.
UsedTo: It is used to emphasize semantic component.
It is used to emphasize references to Russian folk art.
WonBy: It was won by Jager Music Awards.
It was won by Golden Gargoyle Award.
WrittenBy: It was written by Anastasia Kreslina.
It was written by Nikolay Kostilev.
